flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of an adjacency matrix for a graph with V vertices?
flashcards.back: O(V²) as it stores V² entries even for sparse graphs.

flashcards.front: When is an adjacency list preferred over adjacency matrix?
flashcards.back: For sparse graphs (few edges) due to O(V+E) space efficiency.

flashcards.front: What data structure does BFS primarily use?
What is the time complexity of BFS on a connected graph?
How does DFS detect cycles in undirected graphs?
flashcards.back: By checking if adjacent node is visited but not parent.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between BFS and DFS?
flashcards.back: BFS explores level-wise, DFS goes deep first using stack.

flashcards.front: Why is adjacency matrix O(1) for edge queries?
flashcards.back: Direct access via matrix[i][j] to check edge existence.
Unmarking visited nodes when returning from recursion.
Adjacency list with paired (node, weight) entries.
